summary.summary:
The change repurposes the algorithm toggle from useSwiftOref to useJavascriptOref and inverts the runtime branching so the Swift Oref implementation is used by default while the setting now explicitly opts back into the legacy JavaScript implementation. [file:702a23841433] [file:0c4176a1088d] [diff:128cc944edd3]

Function signatures and call sites in OpenAPS and APSManager were updated to accept useJavascriptOref instead of useSwiftOref, and branching logic was flipped so code paths call the Swift implementation when useJavascriptOref is false and the JavaScript implementation when useJavascriptOref is true. [file:0c4176a1088d] [file:a92d7c27bda9] [diff:128cc944edd3]

The persisted settings model and published UI state were renamed from useSwiftOref to useJavascriptOref, with the TrioSettings default value set to false (meaning JavaScript Oref is off by default, i.e., Swift is used). [file:702a23841433] [file:73f9cb8c9ee1] [diff:128cc944edd3]

User-facing labels and localized strings were updated: the control label and mini/verbose hints were changed to "Use JavaScript Oref" and the messages were revised to describe that Swift is now the default and JavaScript is the legacy option. Several localization entries were removed or changed accordingly. [file:742f72ca10ac] [file:a4238cea33ee] [diff:128cc944edd3]

A small UI tag check in LoopStatusView was inverted to reflect the new boolean meaning (it now shows the "Swift Oref" tag when useJavascriptOref is false). [file:34574f60a0b7] [diff:128cc944edd3]

The pull request and commits describe the intent to default users to the Swift implementation so issues can be discovered before removing the JavaScript code. [pr:gordolio/Trio#1273] [pr:nightscout/Trio#1273] [commit:f51bf3f275ff] [commit:531608e39ed1]
summary.userFacingImpact:
Defaults changed so the app will use the Swift Oref algorithm by default and the settings UI now exposes a "Use JavaScript Oref" toggle to opt back into the legacy JavaScript implementation. Users already relying on the JavaScript implementation will need to enable that setting to continue using it. The change also updates on-screen hints and descriptions to explain that Swift is now the default. These impacts are shown in the settings model, UI code, localization, and runtime branching. [file:702a23841433] [file:742f72ca10ac] [file:a4238cea33ee] [file:0c4176a1088d] [file:a92d7c27bda9] [diff:128cc944edd3]

If you need confirmation of any behavioral differences beyond the default/setting inversion (for example performance or numerical differences between Swift and JavaScript implementations), human review and testing are recommended because those effects are not detailed in the supplied changes. [pr:gordolio/Trio#1273] [commit:f51bf3f275ff]
